title: SQL Repositories, Pools, Transactions, and Request Consistency · Language Lens: Http Db Error Mapping
summary: Handlers open a new database handle per request, concatenate resource IDs, leak rows, perform multi-step writes outside transactions, and return before commit ambiguity is resolved. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo http db error mapping increment in context-bound repository and transaction contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map no rows, uniqueness, foreign key, serialization, deadline, cancellation, unavailable, and unknown failures without exposing schema internals.
Define atomic invariants, BeginTx with context and isolation, defer rollback, commit exactly once, and handle PostgreSQL Read Committed changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
